mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing immediate equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and private sector credit growth indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the composite demand shock signal and 60-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in magnitude over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a rise in the industrial production index reflects unmet demand rather than inflationary pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO increases when the manufacturing PMI remains below 50.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand that improves corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansionary quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity valuations and corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market deterioration that forces the Federal Reserve to delay rate cuts, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint labor deterioration signal and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in magnitude over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a surge in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic revitalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no significant positive correlation with HOUST increases when the consumer confidence index remains below its 12-month average.
